CUPERTINO HEALTHCARE & WELLNESS CENTER

Overview

Address
22590 VOSS AVENUE, CUPERTINO, CA 95014
Phone
4082539034
Certified beds
170
Avg daily residents
162 (95% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type

Medicare/Medicaid since
1970-06-20
Setting
Urban
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.83 3.95 3.74 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.43 0.69 0.42 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.93 0.88 0.91 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.48 2.38 2.42 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.35 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.04 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.62
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.27
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.54
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
32.0%
annual
RN turnover
50.0%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.40
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.02
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-12-22 14 12 2 64 1 64
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-05-31 30 8 23 140 1 140
Total weighted health score
83.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (76)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

74 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2025-12-22 2026-01-30
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2025-12-22 2026-01-30
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-12-22 2026-01-30
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2025-12-22 2026-01-30
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. D 2025-12-22 2026-01-30
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-12-22 2026-01-30
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2025-12-22 2026-01-30
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-12-22 2026-01-30
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. D 2025-12-22 2026-01-30
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-12-22 2026-01-30
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. E 2025-12-22 2026-01-30
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-12-22 2026-01-30
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-12-03 2025-12-30
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-06-03 2025-06-12
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-12-17 2025-01-16
0745 Provide medically-related social services to help each resident achieve the highest possible quality of life. D 2024-12-17 2025-01-16
0555 Honor the resident's right to choose his or her attending physician. D 2024-09-11 2024-09-25
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. D 2024-08-27 2024-09-25
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-07-31 2024-08-13
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-06-10 2024-06-25
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-06-10 2024-06-25
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-05-31 2024-06-18
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-05-31 2024-06-18
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-05-31 2024-06-18
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-05-31 2024-06-18
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2024-05-31 2024-06-18
0808 Ensure therapeutic diets are prescribed by the attending physician and may be delegated to a registered or licensed dietitian, to the extent allowed by State law. D 2024-05-31 2024-06-18
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-05-31 2024-06-18
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-05-31 2024-06-18
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2024-04-18 2024-05-01
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-03-26 2024-04-10
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2024-01-09 2024-01-29
0557 Honor the resident's right to be treated with respect and dignity and to retain and use personal possessions. D 2023-10-07 2023-10-27
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2023-10-07 2023-10-27
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2023-09-06 2023-09-26
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2023-09-05 2023-09-20
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. E 2023-07-08 2023-08-06
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2023-07-05 2023-08-02
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-06-06 2023-07-04
0573 Let each resident or the resident's legal representative access or purchase copies of all the resident's records. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-12
0687 Provide appropriate foot care. E 2023-05-16 2023-06-12
0800 Provide each resident with a nourishing, palatable, well-balanced diet that meets his or her daily nutritional and special dietary needs. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-12
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2023-05-09 2023-05-27
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2023-04-17 2023-05-06
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-03-13 2023-04-05
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2021-07-26 2021-09-22
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2021-07-26 2021-09-22
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2021-07-26 2021-09-22
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. E 2021-07-26 2021-09-22
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2021-07-26 2021-09-22
